title: eryngium campestre
intro: Eryngium campestre, also known as field eryngium or sea holly, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its thistle-like flowers and prickly leaves. The medicinal properties of Eryngium campestre include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, reducing pain, and fighting infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Eryngium campestre can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
